eavsr 0.1.0

Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble

Source: R/read.R
read_eavs.Rd

Reads a cached EAVS public-release file into R as a tibble, with EAVS missing-value codes (negative integers) recoded to NA. Downloads the file first if it is not already cached.

Usage

read_eavs(
  cycle,
  level = c("jurisdiction", "raw"),
  clean_names = NULL,
  recode_missing = NULL
)

Arguments

cycle

Integer year of the EAVS cycle.

level

Aggregation level: "jurisdiction" (default, one row per reporting jurisdiction as released by EAC) or "raw" (the file exactly as released, with no recoding).

clean_names

Logical. If TRUE (the default for level = "jurisdiction"), apply janitor::clean_names() to column names.

recode_missing

Logical. If TRUE (the default), recode negative numeric values to NA. EAVS uses negative codes (-99, -77, etc.) to indicate non-response and missing data.

Value

A tibble.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  eavs <- read_eavs(2024)
} # }
